summary: Problem 8 is an open research-level conjecture by Mohammed Abouzaid asking whether a combinatorial condition (exactly 4 faces meeting at each vertex) on a polyhedral Lagrangian surface in R^4 guarantees the existence of a Lagrangian smoothing. With a 9% solvability score and no substantive proof attempt on record, the question remains essentially unaddressed; the single tracked issue was a seed placeholder closed without any proof to evaluate.
problems: No actual proof attempt exists — the best-proof slot shows 100 open issues and the only tracked issue was a placeholder closed with 'no proof to evaluate'.

The core mathematical blocker is unresolved: it is unknown whether the local combinatorial condition (4 faces per vertex) is sufficient to construct a global Hamiltonian isotopy smoothing the polyhedral singularities.

Constructing an explicit Lagrangian smoothing requires controlling the local model at each polyhedral vertex/edge and gluing these into a coherent Hamiltonian isotopy on (0,1] extending continuously to t=0 — no local model has been proposed.
This is a frontier open problem posed by a leading expert; absence of known techniques or partial results makes a complete proof currently out of reach (9% solvability).
Experiment/model provenance is ambiguous ('Experiment: ?', 'claude_opus_5-4'), so there is no traceable prior reasoning to build on.
highlights: The problem statement and definitions (polyhedral Lagrangian surface, Lagrangian smoothing as Hamiltonian isotopy parameterized by (0,1]) are clearly specified, providing a precise target.
The seed placeholder issue has been correctly triaged and closed, leaving a clean slate without false leads.
The conjecture has a sharp combinatorial hypothesis (exactly 4 faces per vertex) that suggests a tractable local-model approach worth investigating.

suggested_todos.title: Survey existing literature on Lagrangian smoothings and tropical/polyhedral Lagrangians (Abouzaid, Mikhalkin) to identify candidate local models at 4-valent vertices
Attempt to construct and verify an explicit local Lagrangian smoothing for a single 4-face vertex in R^4 as a proof-of-concept building block
Investigate obstructions: search for potential counterexamples or topological/symplectic obstructions to gluing local smoothings into a global Hamiltonian isotopy
Open a substantive proof-tracking issue with a structured proof outline (local model, gluing, t=0 limit) replacing the closed placeholder
Clarify experiment/model provenance and record any prior partial reasoning to avoid duplicated effort
